reasoning: The most recent FRED data indicates the 20-year constant maturity TIPS yield dropped sharply in early February, falling from 2.42 on Feb 2 down to a local minimum of 2.24 on Feb 13. Given the short-term random walk behavior of interest rates but a slight tendency for mean-reversion following abrupt drops, the probability of bouncing back above 2.24 over the next week (Feb 22) is slightly higher than 50% (estimated at 55%). Following the standard forecasting principle that directional certainty decays over longer horizons, the baseline forecast for Mar 17 drifts closer to the 50% uncertainty anchor (estimated at 52%).
